== Events == * [[:en:Chōkan|Chōkan]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1163|1163]] [[:en:May 4|May 4]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō]] to [[:en:Chōkan|Chōkan]]. * [[:en:Kōan (Muromachi period)|Kōan]] 1st year / [[:en:Zhengping|Shōhei]] 16th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1361|1361]] [[:en:May 4|May 4]]) – The Northern Court changed its era name from [[:en:Enbun|Enbun]] to [[:en:Kōan (Muromachi period)|Kōan]]. * {{ill|Tenna|ja|天名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1683|1683]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – "Yaoya Oshichi" was executed by immolation at Suzukamori Prison. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1696|1696]] [[:en:April 30|April 30]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate permitted landfill of Edo city waste, which led to the creation of the island now known as {{ill|Eidaijima|ja|永代島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eidaijima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 永代橋|1=Eitai Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549876}}. * {{ill|Xianfeng|ja|咸豊|ko|함풍|uk|Сяньфен|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xianfeng|1=Xianfeng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1135843}} 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1853|1853]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – During the Taiping Rebellion, Hong Xiuquan entered Nanjing, renaming it Tianjing and establishing it as the capital of the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1401|1401]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – [[:en:Emperor Shōkō|Shōkō Tennō]], the 101st Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1428|1428]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1762|1762]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Iizuka Iga-shichi|ja|飯塚伊賀七|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizuka Iga-shichi|1=Igashichi Iizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666171}}, an inventor (died [[:en:1836|1836]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Hirano Kunomi|ja|平野国臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunomi|1=Hirano Kuniomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483648}}, a retainer of the Fukuoka Domain (died [[:en:1864|1864]]).
== Deaths == * [[Shitō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:997|997]] [[:en:May 8|May 8]]) – [[:en:Thái Tông|Taizong of Song]], the 2nd Emperor of the Song dynasty (born [[:en:939|939]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|28|3|30|2|29|4|3|0329|qq=}}

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.

== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1401|1401]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – [[:en:Emperor Shōkō|Shōkō Tennō]], the 101st Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1428|1428]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1762|1762]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Iizuka Iga-shichi|ja|飯塚伊賀七|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizuka Iga-shichi|1=Igashichi Iizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666171}}, an inventor (died [[:en:1836|1836]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Hirano Kunomi|ja|平野国臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunomi|1=Hirano Kuniomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483648}}, a retainer of the Fukuoka Domain (died [[:en:1864|1864]]).

== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1401|1401]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – [[:en:Emperor Shōkō|Shōkō Tennō]], the 101st Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1428|1428]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1762|1762]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Iizuka Iga-shichi|ja|飯塚伊賀七|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizuka Iga-shichi|1=Igashichi Iizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666171}}, an inventor (died [[:en:1836|1836]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Hirano Kunomi|ja|平野国臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunomi|1=Hirano Kuniomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483648}}, a retainer of the Fukuoka Domain (died [[:en:1864|1864]]).
== Deaths == * [[Shitō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:997|997]] [[:en:May 8|May 8]]) – [[:en:Thái Tông|Taizong of Song]], the 2nd Emperor of the Song dynasty (born [[:en:939|939]]).

Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.

== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1401|1401]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – [[:en:Emperor Shōkō|Shōkō Tennō]], the 101st Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1428|1428]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1762|1762]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Iizuka Iga-shichi|ja|飯塚伊賀七|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizuka Iga-shichi|1=Igashichi Iizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666171}}, an inventor (died [[:en:1836|1836]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Hirano Kunomi|ja|平野国臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunomi|1=Hirano Kuniomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483648}}, a retainer of the Fukuoka Domain (died [[:en:1864|1864]]).
== Deaths == * [[Shitō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:997|997]] [[:en:May 8|May 8]]) – [[:en:Thái Tông|Taizong of Song]], the 2nd Emperor of the Song dynasty (born [[:en:939|939]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|28|3|30|2|29|4|3|0329|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月廿九]][[zh:三月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838930]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 29|qq=March 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Chōkan|Chōkan]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1163|1163]] [[:en:May 4|May 4]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō]] to [[:en:Chōkan|Chōkan]]. * [[:en:Kōan (Muromachi period)|Kōan]] 1st year / [[:en:Zhengping|Shōhei]] 16th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1361|1361]] [[:en:May 4|May 4]]) – The Northern Court changed its era name from [[:en:Enbun|Enbun]] to [[:en:Kōan (Muromachi period)|Kōan]]. * {{ill|Tenna|ja|天名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1683|1683]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – "Yaoya Oshichi" was executed by immolation at Suzukamori Prison. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1696|1696]] [[:en:April 30|April 30]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate permitted landfill of Edo city waste, which led to the creation of the island now known as {{ill|Eidaijima|ja|永代島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eidaijima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 永代橋|1=Eitai Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549876}}. * {{ill|Xianfeng|ja|咸豊|ko|함풍|uk|Сяньфен|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xianfeng|1=Xianfeng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1135843}} 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1853|1853]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – During the Taiping Rebellion, Hong Xiuquan entered Nanjing, renaming it Tianjing and establishing it as the capital of the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1401|1401]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – [[:en:Emperor Shōkō|Shōkō Tennō]], the 101st Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1428|1428]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1762|1762]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Iizuka Iga-shichi|ja|飯塚伊賀七|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizuka Iga-shichi|1=Igashichi Iizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666171}}, an inventor (died [[:en:1836|1836]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Hirano Kunomi|ja|平野国臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunomi|1=Hirano Kuniomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483648}}, a retainer of the Fukuoka Domain (died [[:en:1864|1864]]).
== Deaths == * [[Shitō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:997|997]] [[:en:May 8|May 8]]) – [[:en:Thái Tông|Taizong of Song]], the 2nd Emperor of the Song dynasty (born [[:en:939|939]]).

If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.

If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Chōkan|Chōkan]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1163|1163]] [[:en:May 4|May 4]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō]] to [[:en:Chōkan|Chōkan]]. * [[:en:Kōan (Muromachi period)|Kōan]] 1st year / [[:en:Zhengping|Shōhei]] 16th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1361|1361]] [[:en:May 4|May 4]]) – The Northern Court changed its era name from [[:en:Enbun|Enbun]] to [[:en:Kōan (Muromachi period)|Kōan]]. * {{ill|Tenna|ja|天名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1683|1683]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – "Yaoya Oshichi" was executed by immolation at Suzukamori Prison. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1696|1696]] [[:en:April 30|April 30]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate permitted landfill of Edo city waste, which led to the creation of the island now known as {{ill|Eidaijima|ja|永代島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eidaijima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 永代橋|1=Eitai Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549876}}. * {{ill|Xianfeng|ja|咸豊|ko|함풍|uk|Сяньфен|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xianfeng|1=Xianfeng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1135843}} 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1853|1853]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – During the Taiping Rebellion, Hong Xiuquan entered Nanjing, renaming it Tianjing and establishing it as the capital of the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1401|1401]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – [[:en:Emperor Shōkō|Shōkō Tennō]], the 101st Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1428|1428]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1762|1762]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Iizuka Iga-shichi|ja|飯塚伊賀七|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizuka Iga-shichi|1=Igashichi Iizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666171}}, an inventor (died [[:en:1836|1836]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Hirano Kunomi|ja|平野国臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunomi|1=Hirano Kuniomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483648}}, a retainer of the Fukuoka Domain (died [[:en:1864|1864]]).
== Deaths == * [[Shitō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:997|997]] [[:en:May 8|May 8]]) – [[:en:Thái Tông|Taizong of Song]], the 2nd Emperor of the Song dynasty (born [[:en:939|939]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|28|3|30|2|29|4|3|0329|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 29일]][[zh_min_nan:3 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月廿九]][[zh:三月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 29일]][[zh_min_nan:3 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月廿九]][[zh:三月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月廿九]][[zh:三月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838930]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 29|qq=March 29}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.

== Events == * [[:en:Chōkan|Chōkan]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1163|1163]] [[:en:May 4|May 4]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō]] to [[:en:Chōkan|Chōkan]]. * [[:en:Kōan (Muromachi period)|Kōan]] 1st year / [[:en:Zhengping|Shōhei]] 16th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1361|1361]] [[:en:May 4|May 4]]) – The Northern Court changed its era name from [[:en:Enbun|Enbun]] to [[:en:Kōan (Muromachi period)|Kōan]]. * {{ill|Tenna|ja|天名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1683|1683]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – "Yaoya Oshichi" was executed by immolation at Suzukamori Prison. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1696|1696]] [[:en:April 30|April 30]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate permitted landfill of Edo city waste, which led to the creation of the island now known as {{ill|Eidaijima|ja|永代島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eidaijima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 永代橋|1=Eitai Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549876}}. * {{ill|Xianfeng|ja|咸豊|ko|함풍|uk|Сяньфен|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xianfeng|1=Xianfeng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1135843}} 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1853|1853]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – During the Taiping Rebellion, Hong Xiuquan entered Nanjing, renaming it Tianjing and establishing it as the capital of the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1401|1401]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – [[:en:Emperor Shōkō|Shōkō Tennō]], the 101st Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1428|1428]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1762|1762]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Iizuka Iga-shichi|ja|飯塚伊賀七|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizuka Iga-shichi|1=Igashichi Iizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666171}}, an inventor (died [[:en:1836|1836]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Hirano Kunomi|ja|平野国臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunomi|1=Hirano Kuniomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483648}}, a retainer of the Fukuoka Domain (died [[:en:1864|1864]]).
== Deaths == * [[Shitō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:997|997]] [[:en:May 8|May 8]]) – [[:en:Thái Tông|Taizong of Song]], the 2nd Emperor of the Song dynasty (born [[:en:939|939]]).

== Events == * [[:en:Chōkan|Chōkan]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1163|1163]] [[:en:May 4|May 4]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō]] to [[:en:Chōkan|Chōkan]]. * [[:en:Kōan (Muromachi period)|Kōan]] 1st year / [[:en:Zhengping|Shōhei]] 16th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1361|1361]] [[:en:May 4|May 4]]) – The Northern Court changed its era name from [[:en:Enbun|Enbun]] to [[:en:Kōan (Muromachi period)|Kōan]]. * {{ill|Tenna|ja|天名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1683|1683]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – "Yaoya Oshichi" was executed by immolation at Suzukamori Prison. * [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1696|1696]] [[:en:April 30|April 30]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate permitted landfill of Edo city waste, which led to the creation of the island now known as {{ill|Eidaijima|ja|永代島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eidaijima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 永代橋|1=Eitai Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549876}}. * {{ill|Xianfeng|ja|咸豊|ko|함풍|uk|Сяньфен|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xianfeng|1=Xianfeng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1135843}} 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1853|1853]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – During the Taiping Rebellion, Hong Xiuquan entered Nanjing, renaming it Tianjing and establishing it as the capital of the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1401|1401]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – [[:en:Emperor Shōkō|Shōkō Tennō]], the 101st Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1428|1428]]). * [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 12nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1762|1762]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Iizuka Iga-shichi|ja|飯塚伊賀七|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizuka Iga-shichi|1=Igashichi Iizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666171}}, an inventor (died [[:en:1836|1836]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1828|1828]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Hirano Kunomi|ja|平野国臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunomi|1=Hirano Kuniomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483648}}, a retainer of the Fukuoka Domain (died [[:en:1864|1864]]).
== Deaths == * [[Shitō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:997|997]] [[:en:May 8|May 8]]) – [[:en:Thái Tông|Taizong of Song]], the 2nd Emperor of the Song dynasty (born [[:en:939|939]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|28|3|30|2|29|4|3|0329|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Chōkan|en|Chōkan|WD=Q1193904|lt=Chōkan}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1163|en|1163|WD=Q19672|lt=1163}} {{ill|May {{Not to be confused with|March 29|qq=March 29}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Chōkan|en|Chōkan|WD=Q1193904|lt=Chōkan}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1163|en|1163|WD=Q19672|lt=1163}} {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Ōhō|en|Ōhō|WD=Q1193927|lt=Ōhō}} to {{ill|Chōkan|en|Chōkan|WD=Q1193904|lt=Chōkan}}. * {{ill|Kōan (Muromachi period)|en|Kōan (Muromachi period)|WD=Q515424|lt=Kōan}} 1st year / {{ill|Zhengping|en|Zhengping|WD=Q11123413|lt=Shōhei}} 16th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1361|en|1361|WD=Q6159|lt=1361}} {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4}}) – The Northern Court changed its era name from {{ill|Enbun|en|Enbun|WD=Q1145071|lt=Enbun}} to {{ill|Kōan (Muromachi period)|en|Kōan (Muromachi period)|WD=Q515424|lt=Kōan}}. * {{ill|Tenna|ja|天名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1683|en|1683|WD=Q7651|lt=1683}} {{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25}}) – "Yaoya Oshichi" was executed by immolation at Suzukamori Prison. * {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1696|en|1696|WD=Q7699|lt=1696}} {{ill|April 30|en|April 30|WD=Q2536|lt=April 30}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate permitted landfill of Edo city waste, which led to the creation of the island now known as {{ill|Eidaijima|ja|永代島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eidaijima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 永代橋|1=Eitai Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549876}}. * {{ill|Xianfeng|ja|咸豊|ko|함풍|uk|Сяньфен|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xianfeng|1=Xianfeng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1135843}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1853|en|1853|WD=Q7666|lt=1853}} {{ill|May 6|en|May 6|WD=Q2552|lt=May 6}}) – During the Taiping Rebellion, Hong Xiuquan entered Nanjing, renaming it Tianjing and establishing it as the capital of the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom.
== Births == * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei}} 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1401|en|1401|WD=Q6110|lt=1401}} {{ill|May 12|en|May 12|WD=Q2559|lt=May 12}}) – {{ill|Emperor Shōkō|en|Emperor Shōkō|WD=Q451827|lt=Shōkō Tennō}}, the 101st Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1428|en|1428|WD=Q6315|lt=1428}}). * {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1762|en|1762|WD=Q7652|lt=1762}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23}}) – {{ill|Iizuka Iga-shichi|ja|飯塚伊賀七|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizuka Iga-shichi|1=Igashichi Iizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666171}}, an inventor (died {{ill|1836|en|1836|WD=Q7601|lt=1836}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1828|en|1828|WD=Q7570|lt=1828}} {{ill|May 12|en|May 12|WD=Q2559|lt=May 12}}) – {{ill|Hirano Kunomi|ja|平野国臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunomi|1=Hirano Kuniomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483648}}, a retainer of the Fukuoka Domain (died {{ill|1864|en|1864|WD=Q7704|lt=1864}}).
== Deaths == * [[Shitō]] 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|997|en|997|WD=Q23571|lt=997}} {{ill|May 8|en|May 8|WD=Q2555|lt=May 8}}) – {{ill|Thái Tông|en|Thái Tông|WD=Q21406321|lt=Taizong of Song}}, the 2nd Emperor of the Song dynasty (born {{ill|939|en|939|WD=Q23422|lt=939}}).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|28|3|30|2|29|4|3|0329|qq=}}

If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}} then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senshū|WD=Q96759839|1=senshū}}{{efn|ja|先勝|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Chōkan|en|Chōkan|WD=Q1193904|lt=Chōkan|1=Chōkan}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1163|en|1163|WD=Q19672|lt=1163|1=1163}} {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Ōhō|en|Ōhō|WD=Q1193927|lt=Ōhō|1=Ōhō}} to {{ill|Chōkan|en|Chōkan|WD=Q1193904|lt=Chōkan|1=Chōkan}}. * {{ill|Kōan (Muromachi period)|en|Kōan (Muromachi period)|WD=Q515424|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Muromachi period)}} 1st year / {{ill|Zhengping|en|Zhengping|WD=Q11123413|lt=Shōhei|1=Zhengping}} 16th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1361|en|1361|WD=Q6159|lt=1361|1=1361}} {{ill|May 4|en|May 4|WD=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}}) – The Northern Court changed its era name from {{ill|Enbun|en|Enbun|WD=Q1145071|lt=Enbun|1=Enbun}} to {{ill|Kōan (Muromachi period)|en|Kōan (Muromachi period)|WD=Q515424|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Muromachi period)}}. * {{ill|Tenna|ja|天名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1683|en|1683|WD=Q7651|lt=1683|1=1683}} {{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25|1=April 25}}) – "Yaoya Oshichi" was executed by immolation at Suzukamori Prison. * {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1696|en|1696|WD=Q7699|lt=1696|1=1696}} {{ill|April 30|en|April 30|WD=Q2536|lt=April 30|1=April 30}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate permitted landfill of Edo city waste, which led to the creation of the island now known as {{ill|Eidaijima|ja|永代島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eidaijima|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 永代橋|1=Eitai Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549876}}. * {{ill|Xianfeng|ja|咸豊|ko|함풍|uk|Сяньфен|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xianfeng|1=Xianfeng Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1135843}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1853|en|1853|WD=Q7666|lt=1853|1=1853}} {{ill|May 6|en|May 6|WD=Q2552|lt=May 6|1=May 6}}) – During the Taiping Rebellion, Hong Xiuquan entered Nanjing, renaming it Tianjing and establishing it as the capital of the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom.
== Births == * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei|1=Ōei}} 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1401|en|1401|WD=Q6110|lt=1401|1=1401}} {{ill|May 12|en|May 12|WD=Q2559|lt=May 12|1=May 12}}) – {{ill|Emperor Shōkō|en|Emperor Shōkō|WD=Q451827|lt=Shōkō Tennō|1=Emperor Shōkō}}, the 101st Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1428|en|1428|WD=Q6315|lt=1428|1=1428}}). * {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1762|en|1762|WD=Q7652|lt=1762|1=1762}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23|1=April 23}}) – {{ill|Iizuka Iga-shichi|ja|飯塚伊賀七|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizuka Iga-shichi|1=Igashichi Iizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666171}}, an inventor (died {{ill|1836|en|1836|WD=Q7601|lt=1836|1=1836}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1828|en|1828|WD=Q7570|lt=1828|1=1828}} {{ill|May 12|en|May 12|WD=Q2559|lt=May 12|1=May 12}}) – {{ill|Hirano Kunomi|ja|平野国臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunomi|1=Hirano Kuniomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483648}}, a retainer of the Fukuoka Domain (died {{ill|1864|en|1864|WD=Q7704|lt=1864|1=1864}}).
== Deaths == * [[Shitō]] 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|997|en|997|WD=Q23571|lt=997|1=997}} {{ill|May 8|en|May 8|WD=Q2555|lt=May 8|1=May 8}}) – {{ill|Thái Tông|en|Thái Tông|WD=Q21406321|lt=Taizong of Song|1=Thái Tông}}, the 2nd Emperor of the Song dynasty (born {{ill|939|en|939|WD=Q23422|lt=939|1=939}}).

== Births == * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|qid=Q1193078|lt=Ōei|1=Ōei}} 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1401|en|1401|qid=Q6110|lt=1401|1=1401}} {{ill|May 12|en|May 12|qid=Q2559|lt=May 12|1=May 12}}) – {{ill|Emperor Shōkō|en|Emperor Shōkō|qid=Q451827|lt=Shōkō Tennō|1=Emperor Shōkō}}, the 101st Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1428|en|1428|qid=Q6315|lt=1428|1=1428}}). * {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|qid=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} 12nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1762|en|1762|qid=Q7652|lt=1762|1=1762}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|qid=Q2527|lt=April 23|1=April 23}}) – {{ill|Iizuka Iga-shichi|ja|飯塚伊賀七|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizuka Iga-shichi|1=Igashichi Iizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666171}}, an inventor (died {{ill|1836|en|1836|qid=Q7601|lt=1836|1=1836}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|qid=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1828|en|1828|qid=Q7570|lt=1828|1=1828}} {{ill|May 12|en|May 12|qid=Q2559|lt=May 12|1=May 12}}) – {{ill|Hirano Kunomi|ja|平野国臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunomi|1=Hirano Kuniomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483648}}, a retainer of the Fukuoka Domain (died {{ill|1864|en|1864|qid=Q7704|lt=1864|1=1864}}).
== Deaths == * [[Shitō]] 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|997|en|997|qid=Q23571|lt=997|1=997}} {{ill|May 8|en|May 8|qid=Q2555|lt=May 8|1=May 8}}) – {{ill|Thái Tông|en|Thái Tông|qid=Q21406321|lt=Taizong of Song|1=Thái Tông}}, the 2nd Emperor of the Song dynasty (born {{ill|939|en|939|qid=Q23422|lt=939|1=939}}).
